text.title: Framework Decomposition Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns the validator detects. Each has symptom, root-cause, and fix.

antipattern.symptom: Extracted helper has 1 caller; the caller still imports + uses it everywhere; LoC inside the caller barely dropped.
antipattern.root-cause: Extraction was performed mechanically; no reuse target.
antipattern.fix: Extract only when reuse signal exists OR complexity genuinely requires isolation. Otherwise leave inline.

antipattern.symptom: proposed_pattern doesn't match the framework (e.g. service-layer in a React component file).
antipattern.root-cause: Agent learned services from Rails docs and applied them to React.
antipattern.fix: Validator + decision tree route by framework first; reject mismatches. Use custom-hook + selectors for React, service-layer for Rails/Django.
Block. Require either existing >=70% branch coverage OR a characterization-test plan referenced.

antipattern.symptom: target_loc < current_loc but the new code is split into 5 trivial 30-line files that the LLM still has to load all of.
antipattern.root-cause: Author optimized LoC per file without considering "what the LLM loads together".
antipattern.fix: Track ai_context_savings_tokens: actual tokens an LLM would load to make a typical edit. Successful decomposition reduces this number.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: LLM-generated extraction proposals may invent inappropriate patterns ("convert to Redux", "rewrite in TypeScript") that exceed the methodology's scope. Reject any pattern not in the schema enum.
